articles.title: Nat-Gas Prices Fall on Cooler Weather and Ample Supplies

articles.title: US stocks rise as Wall Street shows it's still hungry for AI winners
articles.description: U.S. stocks ticked higher after Wall Street showed its appetite is still big for winners of the artificial-intelligence boom. The S&P 500 rose 0.4% Friday and was on track to close out its fourth winning week in the last five. The Dow Jones Industrial Average added 190 points, and the Nasdaq composite climbed 0.3%. Shares of South Korean tech giant SK Hynix soared in their debut on Wall Street. Oil prices eased, while Treasury yields ticked higher in the bond market. Stock indexes outside the United States were mixed.

articles.title: Federal govt raises petrol, diesel prices in fortnightly review
articles.description: ISLAMABAD: The federal government has increased the prices of petrol and high-speed diesel in its latest fortnightly fuel price review

articles.title: AI Enthusiasm Boosts Wall Street Amid U.S.-Iran Tensions
articles.description: Wall Street indices rose as investors maintained optimism surrounding AI, despite tensions between the U.S. and Iran. SK Hynix's 14% jump in its Nasdaq debut highlighted a strong appetite for AI-related stocks. Oil prices fell slightly, and the Japanese yen strengthened, while U.S. dollar remained stable.

articles.title: Stocks rise, oil prices fall, while South Korean AI darling SK Hynix leaps in its Wall Street debut

articles.title: Asian shares gain despite new US-Iran clashes
articles.description: RECAP: Asian stocks rose sharply yesterday, led by ‌chip and AI firms, as investors brushed off concern about fresh US-Iran hostilities, which has put the spotlight back on oil prices and inflation risk.

articles.title: Govt defends ethanol push: invokes energy needs, farmers, Maruti and Henry Ford
articles.description: The petroleum ministry argued that E20, or 20% ethanol-blended, petrol costs more to produce than the pure one, so it cannot be sold cheaper.

articles.title: Gas prices climbing again as U.S.-Iran tensions flare
articles.description: Prices at the pump sank following a June ceasefire. But renewed conflict in the Middle East and other factors are driving fuel costs back up.
